title: Nephrodium setosum
intro: Nephrodium setosum, also known as the toothed polypody fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium setosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrodium setosum is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in poultices and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Nephrodium setosum is classified under the following groups: Nephrodium is a genus within the family Dryopteridaceae, which belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, division Pteridophyta, and kingdom Plantae. The classification reflects its status as a type of fern. The class Polypodiopsida contains various fern species. The division Pteridophyta is a group of vascular plants with ferns as its primary members. The kingdom Plantae encompasses all plant species, including ferns and flowering plants.
